finding: Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, osteogenesis imperfecta, pseudoxanthoma elasticum

differentials.name: decrease in vision ← increase in intraocular pressure (50%) / cranial nerve deficits = indication for EMERGENT TREATMENT
differentials.raw: decrease in vision ← increase in intraocular pressure (50%) / cranial nerve deficits = indication for EMERGENT TREATMENT

differentials.name: Indirect shunt = communication between dural branch of ICA / ECA + cavernous sinus
differentials.raw: Indirect shunt = communication between dural branch of ICA / ECA + cavernous sinus

differentials.name: benign fistula (Borden type 1) → no cortical venous reflux → no neurologic deficits
differentials.raw: benign fistula (Borden type 1) → no cortical venous reflux → no neurologic deficits

differentials.name: malignant fistula (Borden type 2 & 3) → with cortical venous reflux
differentials.raw: malignant fistula (Borden type 2 & 3) → with cortical venous reflux

differentials.name: focal neurologic symptoms due to venous congestion / rupture of venous pouches;
differentials.raw: focal neurologic symptoms due to venous congestion / rupture of venous pouches;

differentials.name: Draining vein:   enlarged draining vein / capillary bed
differentials.raw: Draining vein:   enlarged draining vein / capillary bed

context: Age:   any Age:   40-60 years; M < F Etiology:   atherosclerosis √  ± visualization of feeding dural branches of ECA / ICA Route of drainage: (a)  superior ophthalmic vein (common) (b)  contralateral cavernous sinus (c)  petrosal sinus (d)  cortical veins (rare) √  dilatation + tortuosity of ipsilateral superior ophthalmic vein, facial veins, internal jugular vein √  enlargement of dural venous sinuses ← increased venous flow + pressure √  enlarged edematous extraocular muscles √  focal asymmetric / diffuse enlargement of cavernous sinus √  occasionally sellar erosion / enlargement √  enlargem
